Bug / Task Description: The logical `&&` operator in the binary-expression evaluator (evalBinaryExpr, pkg/evaluator/evaluator.go) no longer short-circuits: the right-hand side is evaluated even when the left operand is already falsy. Programs that guard a failing or expensive right side behind a falsy left `&&` (e.g. a zero-check before a division) now error or run the side effect instead of resolving to the left value. The value returned for a falsy left is still correct; only the eager evaluation is wrong.
